steps.title: Recognize the one-solution trigger
steps.content:
The phrase exactly one real solution is our trigger to use the discriminant shortcut. When a quadratic equation has exactly one real solution, its discriminant $b^2 - 4ac$ equals zero.

Rearrange the equation to standard form:
$$4x^2 - 32x + (19 - r) = 0$$
Identify the coefficients: $a = 4$, $b = -32$, $c = 19 - r$.

steps.title: Apply the discriminant condition and solve for r
steps.content:
Set the discriminant equal to zero:
$$b^2 - 4ac = 0$$
Substitute the coefficients:
$$(-32)^2 - 4(4)(19 - r) = 0$$
$$1024 - 16(19 - r) = 0$$
$$1024 - 304 + 16r = 0$$
$$720 + 16r = 0$$
$$r = -45$$
